description_long: This is a long-handled paddle featuring a straight, cylindrical shaft made of light-colored wood, transitioning to a broad, flat blade at one end. The blade is rectangular with slightly beveled edges and a uniform brown finish, suggesting it is constructed from a different, possibly denser wood or coated for durability. The handle end is simple and unadorned, with no visible grip enhancements or ergonomic shaping. The shaft appears smooth and consistent in diameter, with a subtle color gradient near the blade, possibly indicating a reinforced joint or a change in material. The overall construction is robust and utilitarian, suitable for use in water sports or as a tool in urban recreational settings. No visible logos, markings, or decorative elements are present, giving the paddle a functional and minimalist appearance.
description: A long, straight wooden paddle with a rectangular brown blade and a simple, unadorned handle. The design is utilitarian and minimalist, with smooth surfaces and no visible markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the paddle, highlighting the broad, flat brown blade and the straight wooden shaft.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the thin profile of the blade and shaft, emphasizing the paddle's narrowness and the subtle color transition near the blade.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, again showing the full length and the rectangular blade, with no additional features visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, with the same thin profile and color gradient visible along the shaft.
attribute_blade_design: rectangular blade with slightly beveled edges, no visible patterns or cutouts
